source.title: Result Note: BNP 1024 — pre-op surgery 5/14, call patient
source.body: BNP 1024 today, significantly elevated from baseline. Patient is scheduled for elective surgery 5/14. Please call patient to assess for symptoms (SOB, weight gain, edema, orthopnea), confirm current diuretic dose, and discuss whether pre-op cardiology clearance needs revisiting. MyChart status Pending — phone call only.

nurseNote:
Pending — Dr. Davis flagged BNP 1024 (significantly elevated). Patient has elective surgery scheduled 5/14. Phone call to assess HF symptoms, diuretic compliance, and pre-op clearance status. MyChart Pending — phone-only contact.

[Note will be completed after phone contact.]

phone.script:
Mrs. Ramirez, this is Brandon from the Cardiology Associates. I am calling about a recent lab result Dr. Davis reviewed today.

Your BNP, which is a blood test that tells us how hard your heart is working, came back higher than usual. Dr. Davis wanted me to check in with you in person before your surgery on May 14th.

First, I would like to ask a few questions:

1. Have you noticed any new shortness of breath, especially lying flat or walking around the house?
2. Has your weight gone up by 2 to 3 pounds or more in a single day recently?
3. Have you noticed any new swelling in your legs or ankles?
4. Are you taking your water pill as prescribed every day?

Depending on your answers, Dr. Davis may want to see you before your surgery, or adjust your medication. We will not change anything without you knowing.

Do you have any questions for me before we start?
phone.voicemail: Hi Mrs. Ramirez, this is Brandon from the Cardiology Associates, calling about a recent lab result. Dr. Davis wanted me to check in with you in person before your surgery on May 14th. Please give us a call back at your earliest convenience at the clinic number. Thank you.

orderPad.note: URGENT — orders pending phone assessment outcome.
